summary: Jeff Atwood argues that the SEO industry is fundamentally dishonest, comparing practitioners to pornographers who exploit prurient interests—except SEOs sell the promise of easy money rather than sex. He cites examples of deliberate system gaming on Digg and quotes Jason Calacanis's criticism that 90% of SEO is 'snake oil.' Atwood contends that legitimate 'white hat' SEO is simply good web design, while the rest is manipulation. Using an analogy of a musician who chased record labels instead of building a genuine fanbase, he concludes that focusing on SEO means prioritizing money over substance, which audiences will eventually recognize.
key_points: SEO practitioners are essentially selling get-rich-quick schemes through search traffic manipulation
The SEOmoz example of deliberately gaming Digg demonstrates the industry's manipulative core
Legitimate 'white hat' SEO is just basic web design principles, not a secret formula
The focus on SEO reveals a prioritization of money and connections over genuine content quality
Relying on SEO tactics is ultimately self-defeating because it neglects authentic audience building
money_quotes: SEOs are the new pornographers of the web.
Just as pornographers sell sex, SEOs sell money. They trade in get-rich-quick schemes via search traffic.
Although SEOs pay lip service to the quality of the content, it's clear that the focus is on one thing, and one thing only: naked, raw greed.
The only difference between SEO 'experts' and pornographers is that pornographers are more honest about what they're actually selling.
Hiring a SEO expert to increase the quality of your site's content is like renting a porn video for the plot and character development.
90% of the SEO market is made up of snake oil salesman.
Steven did not care about the music. Steven cared about the money.
When you focus on SEO, you're focusing on money and connections.
key_insight: Focusing on SEO techniques over genuine content creation reveals a fundamental prioritization of money over substance, and audiences will eventually see through the manipulation.
